rulings.Case Title: KENNETH KINNARD VS. PICK YOUR PARTS DBA THE CITY TOW
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renewed Motion To For Reconsideration-Due To A Clear Error And To Avoid Manifest Inujustice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 10, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF KENNETH KINNARD'S Renewed Motion To For Reconsideration Due To A Clear Error And To Avoid Manifest Injustice. Judge Kahn is unavailable to hear this motion. The Court denies this motion. Plaintiff does not demonstrate good cause for relief under CCP 1008 or any other statute. Plaintiff has had a fair and full opportunity to present his case. It is too late and prejudicial to defendant to vacate the settlement that occurred on April 7, 2010. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATI0N,INC DBA GREAT WESTERN VS. SIMIN SIDDIQ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Creditors Trade Associati0n,Inc Dba Great Western Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 10, 2012, Line 17, Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association, Inc. Dba Great Western Collection Bureau's Application For Right To Attach Order and For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Granted. Creditors Trade Association has established the following" (1) The claim upon which the attachment is based is one upon which an attachment may be issued; (2) The plaintiff has established the probable validity of the claim upon which the attachment is based; (3) The attachment is not sought for a purpose other than the recovery on the claim upon which the attachment is based; and (4) The amount to be secured by the attachment is greater than zero. (CCP § 484.090.) rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Metropolitan Association'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 10, 2012, Line 15, Defendant The Metropolitan Association'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. The Court grants the motion. There is no substantive opposition arguing that the settlement is out of the ballpark within the meaning of Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde Assoc. (1985) 38 Cal.3rd 488. Substantial evidence supports the determination that the settlement is in the ballpark. The Court agrees with objector SD Deacon that this Court's ruling does not affect claims for express indemnity. See CCP 877.6(c). rulings.Case Title: JOHN POPPIN VS. GEORGE V CRESSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 10, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF JOHN POPPIN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel is granted-in-part. Defendant's supplemental response to Form Interrogatory 15.1 is inadequate as to the Seventh, Ninth, Eleventh, Thirteenth, Nineteenth and Twentieth affirmative defenses. It is not a sufficient response to state that "discovery has just begun and is not yet completed" and reserve the right to supplement at a later date. If Defendant does not have personal knowledge sufficient to respond fully to the interrogatory, he must so state in his response. See CCP § 2030.220(c). On the other hand, Defendant's supplemental response to Form Interrogatory 50.5 is adequate because Defendant affirmatively states that he lacks sufficient information in his possession to respond to the interrogatory. See id. With respect to the form interrogatories at issue that ask for the identification of documents, Defendant's response that "[a]ll relevant documents concerning Plaintiff's representation of Infill are in the possession of Plaintiff" is inadequate. Regardless of who possesses the responsive documents, Defendant must identify the responsive documents or affirm that he does not have personal knowledge sufficient to respond to the interrogatory. Defendant's supplemental response to Form Interrogatories 50.1(c), (d) and (f) is adequate because Defendant affirmatively states that he lacks sufficient information in his possession to respond to the interrogatories. Id. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Given the representations in defense counsel's declaration that (a) settlement talks were happening, (b) he was traveling abroad when plaintiff contacted him on October 11, and (c) he was unable to reach his client until October 25, and further given that Defendant has provided supplemental responses to the interrogatories at issue, the Courts does not find that sanctions are warranted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E. BUTLER VS. LAUREEN PARAGUYA DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 10, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT ALLAN PONT M.D, SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS (DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER) DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, without leave to amend. Mr. Butler's claims are barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP § 340.5.) CCP § 340.5 applies to a cause of action for deceit based on the physician's false representations or fraudulent concealment of the nature and extent of the injury. (Weinstock v. Eissler (2002) 224 Cal.App.2d 212, 227.) "Notwithstanding a defendant's continuing efforts to conceal, if plaintiff discovers the claim independently, the limitations period commences." (Sanchez v. South Hoover Hospital (1976) 18 Cal.3d 93, 99.) Mr. Butler admits that he discovered the fraud in August 2008. (Fourth Am. Complaint, p. 4:4-14, 7:9-12.) Further, Mr. Butler alleges that the last overt act that occurred on May 2, 2011 was perpetrated when the California Department of Public Health sending a letter to Mr. Butler. (Fourth Am. Complaint, p. 9:27 - 10:3.) None of the defendants were involved in sending this letter. Lastly, Mr. Butler admits that any claim for patient dumping is barred by the statute of limitations. (Id. at p. 14:19-21.) None of the defendants were involved in sending this letter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E. BUTLER VS. LAUREEN PARAGUYA DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 10, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANTS ALLAN PONT M.D, SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS (DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER) Motion For Monetary Sanctions. Denied. Sutter West errs in moving for sanctions against Mr. Butler per CCP § 128.5 because the statute applies only to complaints or cross-complaints filed prior to December 31, 1994. (CCP § 128.5(b)(1).) Sanctions are not warranted under CCP § 177.5. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ADRIANA URIBE VS. SUTTER HEALTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 10, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANTS SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled as to the second cause of action and sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the third and fifth causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRE PATTERSON VS. CHARLES BYRD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 10, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT CHARLES BYRD'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion is granted because no opposition was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)
